exploration.description: Places where PC interactions focus on mystery and discovery

social.description: Places or situations highlighting interpersonal challenges

traversal.description: Focus on physical obstacles and movement difficulties

event.description: Specific occurrences that drastically impact scene dynamics

bustling_marketplace.name: Bustling Marketplace
bustling_marketplace.description: The economic heart of the settlement, with local artisans, traveling merchants, and patrons across social classes.
impulses: Tempt and tantalize with wares from near and far

passives.name: Tip the Scales
passives.description: PCs can gain advantage on a Presence Roll by offering a handful of gold as part of the interaction.
questions: Will any coin be accepted, or only local currency?
How overt are the PCs in offering this bribe?

actions.name: Unexpected Find
actions.description: Reveal to the PCs that one of the merchants has something they want or need, such as food from their home, a rare book, magical components, a dubious treasure map, or a magical key.
questions: What cost beyond gold will the merchant ask for in exchange for this rarity?

actions.name: Sticky Fingers
actions.description: A thief tries to steal something from a PC. The PC must succeed on an Instinct Roll to notice the thief or lose an item of the GM's choice as the thief escapes to a Close distance.
questions: What drove this person to pickpocketing?
Where is the thief's hideout and how has it avoided notice?

reactions.name: Crowd Closes In
reactions.trigger: When one of the PCs splits from the group
reactions.description: The crowds shift and cut them off from the party.
questions: Where does the crowd's movement carry them?
How do they feel about being alone but surrounded?

ancient_library.name: Ancient Library
ancient_library.description: A vast repository of forgotten knowledge, with towering shelves and mysterious tomes.

passives.name: Overwhelming Knowledge
passives.description: PCs making Knowledge rolls to research information gain advantage but must make a Presence roll (12) or become Dazed by the sheer volume of information.
questions: What specific knowledge overwhelms the character?
How does their mind try to process too much at once?

actions.name: Forbidden Section
actions.description: Reveal a restricted area containing dangerous knowledge. PCs who investigate must make an Instinct roll (15) or suffer consequences from the forbidden lore.
questions: What taboo subject does this section cover?
How does the forbidden knowledge manifest its danger?

reactions.name: Protective Wards
reactions.trigger: When a PC attempts to remove a book or scroll
reactions.description: Ancient protections activate. The PC must make a Knowledge roll (14) to safely handle the item or trigger a magical alarm.
questions: What form do the protective wards take?
Who or what responds to the alarm?

feature_design.passives: Always-active rules that modify how the environment works
feature_design.actions: GM-initiated effects that change the scene (cost Fear for powerful effects)
feature_design.reactions: Triggered responses to PC actions (usually no cost)

creation_guidelines.question_writing: Include 2-3 questions per feature to help GMs customize and expand on the basic effect
